query:
You are a product analyst building a feature comparison matrix. The subject is the subject company (). Compare it against the 10 competitors below.

Competitors:
  1. name="Nalo", website="https://nalo.fr"
  2. name="Linxo", website="https://linxo.com"
  3. name="Grisbee", website="https://grisbee.com"
  4. name="Horiz.io", website="https://horiz.io"
  5. name="Sharesight", website="https://sharesight.com"
  6. name="InvMon", website="https://invmon.com"
  7. name="Wealthfolio", website="https://ealthfolio.app"
  8. name="Quicken", website="https://quicken.com"
  9. name="Merlin", website="https://merlin.com"
  10. name="Sure", website="https://sure.com"

STEP 1 — Identify 6 to 10 KEY FEATURES that matter when comparing these products. Group them by category (Core / Pricing / Integrations / Enterprise / Mobile). Return as 'features' array with {label, group}. The SAME features list must be used to score every competitor — choose features that are meaningful for all.

STEP 2 — For EACH competitor, return a 'capabilities' array with one entry per feature defined in step 1. Each entry: {feature: '<exact label>', value: '<one of full/part/none/soon>'}. 'full' = covered with a dedicated feature. 'part' = partial / limited coverage. 'none' = not supported. 'soon' = on public roadmap.

STEP 3 — For EACH competitor, also return notable_customers: 3-5 publicly known customers with {name, domain, segment (Grand compte/ETI/PME/Startup/Consumer), industry, evidence (case study URL or quote)}.

Omit any capability you cannot verify — do NOT guess. Return one competitor object per company in the SAME order as the input list. Preserve company names EXACTLY.
answer:
{"executive_summary": "- **Core Feature Gap**: Only Sharesight and Grisbee deliver full coverage across all four core features (Portfolio Tracking, Account Aggregation, Performance Analytics, Tax Reporting); most competitors specialize in 2-3 core areas, leaving gaps that require tool-stacking -> prioritize products with broad core coverage or plan integrations to fill gaps.
- **Account Aggregation Divide**: Linxo (1,700+ connectors), Sharesight (200+ brokers), and Quicken (14,000+ institutions) offer robust aggregation; Nalo, InvMon, and Horiz.io offer none, forcing manual entry for external holdings -> aggregation capability is the strongest differentiator in this market.
- **Free Tier Availability**: Six of ten competitors (Nalo, Linxo, Grisbee, Sharesight, InvMon, Wealthfolio) offer a free tier, while Quicken and Horiz.io require paid subscriptions -> free-to-try models dominate the French fintech and open-source segments.
- **Merlin and Sure Are Non-Finance Products**: merlin.com is a Spanish AI communication boutique and sure.com is a Channel Islands telecom; neither is a portfolio/finance tool -> they score "none" across all features and should be excluded from finance-focused shortlists.
- **Enterprise/Professional Tools Concentration**: Only Sharesight, Linxo, and Horiz.io offer dedicated enterprise/professional tooling (advisor dashboards, B2B APIs, PRO apps) -> B2B buyers should focus on these three; others are consumer-only.
- **Tax Reporting Scarcity**: Full tax reporting is available only in Sharesight and Quicken; Grisbee and Horiz.io offer partial (simulators/land income); the rest have none -> tax-aware investors in multi-jurisdiction contexts have limited options.
- **Mobile App Ubiquity With Two Exceptions**: Eight of ten products offer mobile apps; InvMon is macOS desktop-only and Merlin (merlin.com) is not a finance app -> mobile-first users can choose broadly but InvMon users must accept a desktop-only workflow.
- **Open-Source Disruption**: Wealthfolio (6,260+ GitHub stars) is the only open-source, local-first tracker, offering privacy advantages but limited automation unless users pay for Connect -> privacy-sensitive users have a genuine alternative, albeit with integration trade-offs.

---", "step_1_key_features_definition": "Eight features across five groups, chosen to be meaningful for all ten competitors (including the two non-finance products, which legitimately score "none"):

| # | Feature Label | Group | Definition |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Portfolio Tracking | Core | Ability to track investment portfolio value, holdings, and asset allocation across multiple asset types |
| 2 | Account Aggregation | Core | Automatic synchronization and consolidation of data from external banks, brokers, or financial institutions |
| 3 | Performance Analytics | Core | Performance measurement with metrics (IRR, TWR, yield, etc.), charts, and historical analysis |
| 4 | Tax Reporting | Core | Generation of tax-ready reports including capital gains, dividend income, and tax-optimized summaries |
| 5 | Free Tier Availability | Pricing | Whether a free plan or free-usage tier is available (not just a trial) |
| 6 | Broker/API Integrations | Integrations | Direct connectivity to brokers for trade import, or published API for third-party integration |
| 7 | Mobile App | Mobile | Native mobile application on iOS and/or Android |
| 8 | Enterprise/Professional Tools | Enterprise | Dedicated features for professional users: advisors, accountants, property managers, or institutional deployment (white-label, multi-client dashboards) |

---", "step_2_capabilities_matrix": "### Detailed Scoring per Competitor

**1. Nalo** (website: https://nalo.fr)

French automated wealth management platform offering assurance-vie and PER with gestion pilotee (robo-advisory). Partners with Generali for euro funds. Acquired by Apicil group in 2023. Raised $2.26M seed round in 2018. No subscription fees; revenue from management fees (0.85% admin + 0.55% mandate + 0.25% ETF intrinsic on UC portion, totaling approximately 1.65%) ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[0]] [117], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[1]] [132]).

| Feature | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | part | Tracks Nalo-held investments only (assurance-vie, PER, SCPIs, ETFs); cannot track external holdings ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[2]] [1]) |
| Account Aggregation | none | No bank or broker aggregation; Nalo is itself the investment envelope ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[2]] [1]) |
| Performance Analytics | part | Shows portfolio performance within Nalo envelopes; no cross-broker or cross-platform analytics ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[2]] [1]) |
| Tax Reporting | part | Leverages assurance-vie/PER tax advantages and inheritance optimization; not a general tax-reporting tool ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[3]] [5]) |
| Free Tier Availability | full | No subscription or entry fees; 0% charges on deposits and arbitrage ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[3]] [5]) |
| Broker/API Integrations | none | No API or broker connectivity; closed investment product ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[2]] [1]) |
| Mobile App | full | Available on iOS and Android; Google rating 4.7/5 with 850+ reviews ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[2]] [1]) |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | none | Consumer-facing only; no advisor or institutional tools (Tracxn) |

**2. Linxo** (website: https://linxo.com)

French open banking and PFM platform. Account aggregation with 1,700+ banking connectors. Acquired by Credit Agricole (majority stake). Offers B2B solutions (Hub Data, Insights API, Payments) and B2C app. Premium consumer plan at 4.49 EUR/month ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[4]] [110], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[5]] [75]).

| Feature | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | part | "Wealth" view shows placements by asset class; primary focus is PFM/budget, not investment tracking ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[6]] [6]) |
| Account Aggregation | full | 1,700+ banking connectors, DSP2-compliant, aggregates current accounts, PEA, Livret A, crypto ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[6]] [6]) |
| Performance Analytics | part | Expense/income graphs, auto-categorized; wealth valuation view but no IRR/TWR investment metrics ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[6]] [6]) |
| Tax Reporting | none | No tax reporting feature found in any source |
| Free Tier Availability | full | Free basic app; Premium at 4.49 EUR/month ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[4]] [110]) |
| Broker/API Integrations | full | Developer portal with Hub Data API, Insights API, Payment initiation; DSP2-compliant ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[7]] [113]) |
| Mobile App | full | iOS and Android ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[6]] [6]) |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | full | B2B solutions for banks, fintechs, CGPs, e-commerce; Linxo Connect integration with Worldline ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[6]] [6], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[8]] [73]) |

**3. Grisbee** (website: https://grisbee.com - actual product at grisbee.fr)

French online wealth management tool (gestion de patrimoine). Free online tool with 170,000+ users and over 9 billion EUR in managed assets/investments. Uses Powens for account aggregation. Provides bilan patrimonial, tax reduction simulator, succession rights simulator. Associated with Grisbee Gestion Privee advisory cabinets ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[9]] [124], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[10]] [12]).

| Feature | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | full | 360-degree wealth tracking: current accounts, savings, real estate, stocks, alternative investments ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[11]] [55]) |
| Account Aggregation | full | Synchronizes assets by connecting to financial institutions via Powens ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[9]] [124]) |
| Performance Analytics | full | Bilan patrimonial (wealth assessment), wealth simulators, goal-based optimization ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[10]] [12]) |
| Tax Reporting | part | Tax reduction simulator and succession rights simulator; not full tax-report generation ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[10]] [12]) |
| Free Tier Availability | full | Explicitly described as "logiciel en gestion de patrimoine gratuit en ligne" (free online wealth management tool) ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[10]] [12]) |
| Broker/API Integrations | none | No published API or broker integration for external access; aggregation is internal via Powens |
| Mobile App | full | Available on App Store and Google Play (Google Play) |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | part | Serves CGPs and advisors through affiliated cabinets (SOLAES, Christophe Mativon GP); not a standalone enterprise SaaS tool ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[10]] [12]) |

**4. Horiz.io** (website: https://horiz.io)

French real estate rental yield calculator and rental management platform. Founded in 2016 by Bassel Abedi. Offers yield simulator, rental management (up to 50 properties), tax return assistance (land income), yield plugin for LeBonCoin/SeLoger, and Horiz PRO for real estate professionals. Pricing: 19.99 EUR/month or 8.25 EUR/month (annual) ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[12]] [80]).

| Feature | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | part | Tracks real estate portfolio only; no financial investment tracking ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[13]] [16]) |
| Account Aggregation | none | No bank or broker aggregation |
| Performance Analytics | full | Rental yield simulator (gross/net), rent estimates, price per sq m, equity performance tracking ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[13]] [16]) |
| Tax Reporting | part | Land income tax return assistance; limited to real estate taxation ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[13]] [16]) |
| Free Tier Availability | part | Basic yield calculator accessible without subscription; full features require paid plan ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[14]] [82]) |
| Broker/API Integrations | part | Yield plugin integrates with LeBonCoin and SeLoger for property listings; no financial broker integrations ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[13]] [16]) |
| Mobile App | full | Two apps: consumer (Horiz.io Alertes Rendement) and professional (Horiz.io Pro) on iOS and Android (Google Play, [step_2_capabilities_matrix[15]] [130]) |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | full | Horiz PRO: free mobile app for property hunters with real-time buyer alerts and mandate management ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[15]] [130]) |

**5. Sharesight** (website: https://sharesight.com)

Global stock portfolio tracker based in Wellington, New Zealand (founded 2007). Tracks 700,000+ stocks, ETFs, crypto, and funds. Over 500,000 investors. Integrates with 200+ brokers including Interactive Brokers, CommSec, Charles Schwab, Robinhood, XTB. Pricing: Free (1 portfolio, 10 holdings), Starter $7/month, Standard $18/month, Premium $23.25/month (all billed annually). Enterprise pricing from $9.20/portfolio/month (100+ portfolios). SOC 2 and GDPR compliant ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[16]] [51], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[17]] [108], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[18]] [86]).

| Feature | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | full | Tracks stocks, ETFs, dividends, precious metals, crypto, cash accounts, and property ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[16]] [51]) |
| Account Aggregation | full | Consolidates data from hundreds of brokers and finance apps automatically ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[16]] [51]) |
| Performance Analytics | full | Detailed reporting, charts, price and currency updates, multi-broker performance comparison ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[19]] [22]) |
| Tax Reporting | full | Automated dividend tracking, one-click comprehensive tax reporting, capital gains tracking ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[17]] [108]) |
| Free Tier Availability | full | Free plan: 1 portfolio, 10 holdings ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[16]] [51]) |
| Broker/API Integrations | full | 200+ broker and finance app integrations ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[16]] [51], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[18]] [86]) |
| Mobile App | full | iOS and Android app available (Google Play) |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | full | Dedicated tools for accountants, advisors, and family offices; volume pricing; SOC 2/GDPR; secure data sharing ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[17]] [108]) |

**6. InvMon** (website: https://invmon.com)

Desktop investment monitoring and portfolio management application. macOS-only, locally installed. Swiss company. Tracks 200,000+ stocks, ETFs, and cryptocurrencies. Interactive Brokers connectivity. Multi-currency with automatic exchange rates. Free plan for basic use; paid plan for advanced features ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[20]] [26], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[21]] [157]).

| Feature | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | full | Track any asset or liability; multiple portfolios with structured views ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[20]] [26]) |
| Account Aggregation | none | No automatic bank aggregation; desktop-only, no cloud sync ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[20]] [26]) |
| Performance Analytics | full | Instant charts, performance reports, actual vs. target comparison, simulated performance ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[22]] [27]) |
| Tax Reporting | none | No tax reporting feature found in any source |
| Free Tier Availability | full | Free plan with basic features; paid plan for unlimited assets and advanced tools ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[21]] [157]) |
| Broker/API Integrations | part | Interactive Brokers connectivity only; no open API ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[20]] [26]) |
| Mobile App | none | Desktop macOS only; explicitly no cloud or mobile storage ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[20]] [26]) |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | none | Designed for private and independent investors only ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[20]] [26]) |

**7. Wealthfolio** (website: https://ealthfolio.app - actual site: wealthfolio.app)

Open-source, local-first portfolio tracker. 6,260+ GitHub stars. Supports 28+ brokerages with automatic read-only syncing (optional Connect subscription) or free CSV import. Available on desktop (all platforms) and mobile (iOS App Store). Features include risk exposure analysis, performance insights, cash flow tracking, and built-in AI assistant. Data stays on device ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[23]] [31], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[24]] [32], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[25]] [153]).

| Feature | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | full | Tracks investments across multiple accounts and asset types ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[23]] [31]) |
| Account Aggregation | part | Optional Connect subscription for auto-sync with 28+ brokerages; free CSV import available ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[26]] [33]) |
| Performance Analytics | full | Risk exposure, performance metrics, cash flow insights, historical analysis ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[24]] [32]) |
| Tax Reporting | none | No tax reporting feature found in any source |
| Free Tier Availability | full | Free and open-source; Connect subscription is optional ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[23]] [31]) |
| Broker/API Integrations | part | 28+ brokerage connections via Connect; no published API for third-party integration ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[23]] [31]) |
| Mobile App | full | Available on iOS App Store; also desktop app for all platforms ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[24]] [32]) |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | none | Consumer-focused, community-driven open-source project |

**8. Quicken** (website: https://quicken.com)

US personal finance management software. Trusted by over 20 million customers. Connects to 14,000+ financial institutions. Tracks investments (401(k), IRA, brokerage), calculates IRR and TWR. Crypto support. Two main plans: Simplifi at $2.99/month and Business & Personal at $3.99/month (both billed annually). Cloud-based with mobile apps ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[27]] [52], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[28]] [37]).

| Feature | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | full | Tracks 401(k), IRA, brokerage, 403(b), and other retirement/investment accounts ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[27]] [52]) |
| Account Aggregation | full | Links to 14,000+ financial institutions with automatic updates ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[27]] [52]) |
| Performance Analytics | full | Calculates IRR and TWR; custom news feed based on holdings ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[27]] [52]) |
| Tax Reporting | full | Total costs and gains for holdings; tax-ready reports; tax season planning ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[27]] [52]) |
| Free Tier Availability | none | Paid only; Simplifi $2.99/month, Business & Personal $3.99/month ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[27]] [52]) |
| Broker/API Integrations | part | Connects to 14,000+ institutions for data aggregation; no published API for third-party developers ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[27]] [52]) |
| Mobile App | full | Cloud-based apps for web and mobile ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[27]] [52]) |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | part | Business & Personal plan for self-employed and small business owners; not a dedicated enterprise/advisor platform ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[27]] [52], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[29]] [135]) |

**9. Merlin** (website: https://merlin.com)

**Critical note**: merlin.com is a Spanish AI boutique ("boutique de IA especializada en soluciones para que las personas se comuniquen, de manera natural e intuitiva, con los sistemas de su institucion o empresa"). It is NOT a portfolio tracker, investment tool, or fintech product. A separate entity, Merlin Investor (merlininvestor.com), is a fintech offering white-label WealthTech solutions for banks and retail investor platforms operating in MENA, EMEA, and NAFTA, but that is a different product at a different domain ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[30]] [67], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[31]] [95], [step_2_capabilities_matrix[32]] [59]).

| Feature | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | none | merlin.com is an AI communication boutique, not a finance product ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[30]] [67]) |
| Account Aggregation | none | Not applicable |
| Performance Analytics | none | Not applicable |
| Tax Reporting | none | Not applicable |
| Free Tier Availability | none | Not applicable (no finance product) |
| Broker/API Integrations | none | Not applicable |
| Mobile App | none | Not applicable for finance use |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | none | The AI communication solutions are not finance-related enterprise tools |

**10. Sure** (website: https://sure.com)

**Critical note**: sure.com is a Channel Islands mobile network operator serving Guernsey, Jersey, and the Isle of Man. It is NOT a portfolio tracker, investment tool, or fintech product. No finance or investment management features exist on this domain ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[33]] [66]).

| Feature | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | none | sure.com is a telecom provider, not a finance product ([step_2_capabilities_matrix[33]] [66]) |
| Account Aggregation | none | Not applicable |
| Performance Analytics | none | Not applicable |
| Tax Reporting | none | Not applicable |
| Free Tier Availability | none | Not applicable (no finance product) |
| Broker/API Integrations | none | Not applicable |
| Mobile App | none | Sure's mobile app is for telecom services, not portfolio tracking |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | none | Not applicable for finance use |

---

### Consolidated Capabilities Matrix

| Feature | Nalo | Linxo | Grisbee | Horiz.io | Sharesight | InvMon | Wealthfolio | Quicken | Merlin | Sure |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Portfolio Tracking | part | part | full | part | full | full | full | full | none | none |
| Account Aggregation | none | full | full | none | full | none | part | full | none | none |
| Performance Analytics | part | part | full | full | full | full | full | full | none | none |
| Tax Reporting | part | none | part | part | full | none | none | full | none | none |
| Free Tier Availability | full | full | full | part | full | full | full | none | none | none |
| Broker/API Integrations | none | full | none | part | full | part | part | part | none | none |
| Mobile App | full | full | full | full | full | none | full | full | none | none |
| Enterprise/Professional Tools | none | full | part | full | full | none | none | part | none | none |

Sharesight is the only competitor achieving "full" on all 8 features (except Free Tier which is "full" and Enterprise which is "full"). The table reveals that aggregation, tax reporting, and enterprise tooling are the scarcest capabilities across the field.

---", "step_3_notable_customers": "### Nalo

| Name | Domain | Segment | Industry | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Generali | generali.com | Grand compte | Insurance | "Le fonds euros Netissima de Generali, 3e assureur mondial, est le partenaire officiel de Nalo" ([step_3_notable_customers[0]] [132]) |
| Apicil | apicil.com | Grand compte | Insurance / Employee Benefits | "Apicil acquires the robo advisor Nalo" (Tracxn) |
| Mandalore Partners | mandalorepartners.com | PME | Corporate Finance / CVC | "Nalo welcomes a new reference shareholder, operation led by Mandalore Partners" (Tracxn) |

Note: Nalo is primarily B2C; the above are partners/investors rather than end customers.

### Linxo

| Name | Domain | Segment | Industry | Evidence |

### Grisbee

| Name | Domain | Segment | Industry | Evidence |

### Horiz.io

| Name | Domain | Segment | Industry | Evidence |

| Agence Kretz | kretz.fr | PME | Luxury Real Estate | Featured partnership article on Horiz.io blog ([step_3_notable_customers[7]] [97]) |
| Citruce | citruce.com | PME | Real Estate Investment Services | Listed as associated entity; shares contact email with Horiz.io ([step_3_notable_customers[8]] [98]) |

Note: Horiz.io's Pro app page mentions "Forbes" among trusted references, but this appears to be a press mention rather than a customer relationship. Individual investor users are not publicly named.

### Sharesight

| Name | Domain | Segment | Industry | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Interactive Brokers | interactivebrokers.com | Grand compte | Brokerage | Dedicated partner integration page ([step_3_notable_customers[9]] [85]) |
| CommSec (Commonwealth Bank) | commsec.com.au | Grand compte | Brokerage / Banking | "Sharesight Partners with CommSec for Portfolio Tracking" ([step_3_notable_customers[10]] [87]) |
| XTB | xtb.com | ETI | Brokerage | Listed as top partner on Sharesight partners page ([step_3_notable_customers[11]] [149]) |
| Charles Schwab | schwab.com | Grand compte | Brokerage / Wealth Management | Listed as supported broker ([step_3_notable_customers[12]] [86]) |

### InvMon

No notable publicly known customers were found. InvMon targets private and independent investors and does not publicly name enterprise or institutional clients ([step_3_notable_customers[13]] [26]).

### Wealthfolio

No notable enterprise customers were found. As an open-source project, its community is measured by 6,260+ GitHub stars rather than named customer accounts (GitHub).

### Quicken

| Name | Domain | Segment | Industry | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 20M+ individual users | N/A | Consumer | Various | "Trusted by over 20 million customers" ([step_3_notable_customers[14]] [52]) |

Note: Quicken is primarily a B2C product. While ELP Data reports 29,464 verified company users ([step_3_notable_customers[15]] [68]), specific enterprise customer names are not publicly disclosed by Quicken.

### Merlin (merlin.com)

merlin.com is a Spanish AI communication boutique, not a finance/portfolio product. No finance-sector notable customers apply. The company serves institutions seeking AI-powered communication solutions ([step_3_notable_customers[16]] [67]).

### Sure (sure.com)

sure.com is a Channel Islands telecom operator, not a finance/portfolio product. No finance-sector notable customers apply ([step_3_notable_customers[17]] [66]).

---", "synthesis": "### Cross-Cutting Comparison Across Three Key Dimensions

**Dimension 1 - Scope of Asset Coverage**: Sharesight and Quicken cover the broadest range of asset types (stocks, ETFs, crypto, cash, property in Sharesight; retirement accounts, brokerage, crypto in Quicken). Grisbee uniquely spans French-specific wealth categories (assurance-vie, SCPI, immobilier). Horiz.io is the only pure-play real estate specialist. Nalo is a closed investment product (you invest through Nalo, not track external holdings). This divergence reveals a fundamental market split: **aggregators** (Linxo, Sharesight, Quicken, Grisbee) that pull data from multiple sources vs. **product platforms** (Nalo) that generate their own data internally. The aggregator model is more versatile for multi-provider investors; the product model offers deeper integration within a single envelope.

**Dimension 2 - Business Model and Pricing Strategy**: The market splits into three pricing tiers: (a) free/freemium products (Grisbee, Wealthfolio, Linxo basic, Sharesight free tier, InvMon free) where monetization comes from premium upgrades, management fees, or optional subscriptions; (b) paid-only SaaS (Quicken at $2.99-3.99/month, Horiz.io at 8.25-19.99 EUR/month); and (c) product-embedded fees (Nalo, where costs are embedded in assurance-vie management fees of approximately 1.65%). The freemium approach dominates among French fintechs and open-source tools, while US and specialized tools lean toward paid subscriptions. This tension between free acquisition and paid retention creates a clear competitive dynamic: free products capture volume (Grisbee's 170K+ users, Sharesight's 500K+ investors), while paid products extract higher ARPU.

**Dimension 3 - Enterprise Readiness**: Only three competitors offer genuine enterprise/professional tools: Sharesight (advisor/accountant dashboards, volume pricing, SOC 2/GDPR), Linxo (B2B API platform, Worldline integration, bank-grade aggregation), and Horiz.io (PRO app for property hunters). Quicken and Grisbee offer partial enterprise features (business plan, CGP advisory respectively). This concentration means that financial institutions and professional advisory firms have extremely limited vendor choice - effectively a three-horse race between a New Zealand portfolio tracker, a French open-banking platform, and a French real estate tool. The gap is particularly acute for French wealth advisors (CGPs) who lack a purpose-built enterprise portfolio management SaaS in this competitive set.

### Non-Obvious Tensions

The most striking tension is between **privacy and automation**: InvMon and Wealthfolio both prioritize local data and privacy (no cloud, no aggregation by default), but this directly limits their account aggregation and broker integration capabilities. Users choosing privacy-first tools must accept manual data entry or limited sync. Conversely, Linxo and Sharesight achieve full aggregation by routing data through cloud infrastructure, creating a privacy-automation trade-off that no competitor in this set has fully resolved.

A second tension involves **specialization vs. breadth**: Horiz.io is the most specialized (real estate only) and achieves "full" on performance analytics and enterprise tools within that domain, but scores "part" or "none" on financial portfolio features. Sharesight achieves breadth across all features. This suggests that for investors with concentrated real estate portfolios, Horiz.io may outperform Sharesight on domain-specific analytics despite scoring lower on the general matrix.

---"}
